seoTitle: Recom Power Solutions | Industrial & Medical Power System Design
seoDescription: Explore Recom power solutions for industrial automation and medical equipment. Complete power system designs using DC-DC converters and AC-DC supplies with technical support from authorized distributor.

faqs.question: How do I select the right Recom power solution for my application?
faqs.answer: Selecting the right Recom power solution involves: 1) Define your power requirements - voltage, current, and total power; 2) Identify your application - industrial, medical, automotive, or commercial; 3) Consider isolation requirements - medical applications need 2xMOPP isolation; 4) Evaluate efficiency needs - Recom offers up to 90% efficiency; 5) Review certification requirements - UL, CE, medical safety standards; 6) Assess environmental conditions - temperature range, vibration, humidity. Our FAE team can help assess your requirements and recommend the optimal solution architecture.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for power solution assessment and architecture recommendations.

faqs.question: What support does BeiLuo provide for Recom solution implementation?
faqs.answer: BeiLuo provides comprehensive support for Recom solution implementation: Technical consultation - power architecture design and component selection; Application engineering - schematic review, PCB layout guidance, thermal analysis; Prototype support - evaluation boards, samples, and testing assistance; Production support - BOM optimization, supply chain management, and quality assurance. Our FAE team has extensive experience with Recom products and can guide you through the entire design process from concept to production.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our technical support team for solution implementation assistance.

faqs.question: Can Recom solutions be customized for specific requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, Recom solutions can be customized to meet specific requirements: Standard modules can be combined in various configurations; Custom pinouts and packages available for volume applications; Modified specifications for specific voltage combinations; Special testing and screening for high-reliability applications. Customization process: Define requirements with our FAE team; Review proposed solution architecture; Validate with prototypes and testing; Transition to production with full support. Contact us to discuss your specific customization needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team to discuss customization options for your specific requirements.

faqs.question: What is the typical lead time for Recom power solutions?
faqs.answer: Lead times for Recom power solutions vary based on configuration: Standard catalog products: 2-4 weeks from factory; Modified standard products: 4-6 weeks including special testing; Custom configurations: 6-10 weeks for design and validation; High-volume production: 4-6 weeks with scheduled deliveries. As an authorized distributor, BeiLuo maintains inventory of popular Recom products for immediate delivery. For production programs, we recommend establishing forecasts and supply agreements to ensure continuous availability.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our sales team for current lead times and project timeline planning.

faqs.question: How do Recom power solutions compare to discrete designs?
faqs.answer: Recom modular solutions offer significant advantages over discrete designs: Time to market - pre-validated modules reduce design time from months to weeks; Reliability - factory-tested modules with proven field performance; Efficiency - optimized designs achieve up to 90% efficiency; Compact size - high power density in standard packages; Certifications - pre-certified to international safety standards; Support - comprehensive technical documentation and FAE support. While discrete designs may offer lower component cost, the total cost of ownership typically favors modular solutions when considering design time, reliability, and time-to-market.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for a detailed comparison of Recom solutions vs discrete designs.

solutions.name: Industrial Automation Power Solution
solutions.title: Industrial Automation Power Solution
solutions.description: Reliable power system for PLCs, sensors, and industrial control equipment
solutions.longDescription: The Industrial Automation Power Solution leverages Recom's robust DC-DC converters and AC-DC power supplies to deliver reliable power for industrial control systems. This solution features wide input voltage ranges to accommodate factory power variations, high isolation for noise immunity in electrically harsh environments, and extended temperature operation for reliability in industrial settings. The modular architecture allows flexible configuration for various industrial applications from simple sensor power to complex PLC systems.
benefits: Wide input voltage range handles factory power variations
High isolation provides noise immunity in industrial environments
Extended temperature range ensures reliability in harsh conditions
Compact modules enable high-density control panel designs
Long MTBF reduces maintenance and downtime costs

coreAdvantages.title: Wide Input Range
coreAdvantages.description: 4:1 input voltage range accommodates 12V, 24V, and 48V industrial power systems with voltage variations.

coreAdvantages.title: High Isolation
coreAdvantages.description: 1600VDC to 6000VDC isolation protects sensitive control circuits from industrial noise and transients.

coreAdvantages.title: Industrial Temperature
coreAdvantages.description: Full power operation from -40°C to +85°C without derating for reliable performance in harsh environments.

coreAdvantages.title: High Reliability
coreAdvantages.description: MTBF exceeding 1.5 million hours ensures long-term operation with minimal maintenance.

coreAdvantages.title: Compact Design
coreAdvantages.description: High power density in standard SIP and DIP packages enables space-efficient control panel designs.

components.description: AC-DC power supplies for 12V and 24V bus generation
Isolated DC-DC converters for analog and digital circuit separation
Non-isolated buck regulators for efficient point-of-load conversion

customerCases.application: PLC power system for production line control
customerCases.challenge: Needed reliable power for PLCs in a high-temperature factory environment with significant electrical noise from motor drives and welding equipment.
customerCases.solution: Implemented isolated DC-DC converters with 1600VDC isolation and wide temperature range. Used 4:1 input converters to handle 24V bus variations.
customerCases.result: Achieved 99.9% uptime over 3 years, zero field failures. System operated reliably at 70°C ambient in sealed enclosures.

customerCases.application: Sensor power for hazardous area monitoring
customerCases.challenge: Required intrinsically safe power for sensors in Class I Div 2 areas with wide temperature variations from -20°C to +60°C.
customerCases.solution: Deployed compact SIP converters with wide temperature range and high isolation. Implemented redundant power paths for critical sensors.
customerCases.result: Met all safety requirements, passed hazardous area certification. System has operated for 5 years without power-related failures.

faeInsights.insight: Industrial automation power design requires careful attention to noise immunity and reliability. Recom's high-isolation converters are excellent for breaking ground loops between noisy motor drives and sensitive control electronics. The wide input ranges are particularly valuable in factories where 24V bus voltages can vary significantly due to long cable runs and load switching.
faeInsights.logic: Industrial power design follows these principles: First, use AC-DC supplies with universal input for flexibility. Second, implement isolated DC-DC converters to separate noisy and sensitive circuits. Third, select 4:1 input range converters to handle voltage variations. Fourth, ensure adequate derating for high-temperature environments. Fifth, add protection against transients and reverse polarity.
keyTakeaways: High isolation (1600VDC+) is essential for noise immunity
4:1 input range handles factory power variations
Wide temperature range ensures reliability in harsh environments
Compact modules enable high-density panel designs
commonPitfalls: Insufficient isolation between noisy and sensitive circuits
Not accounting for voltage drops in long cable runs
Inadequate thermal design for sealed enclosures
Poor grounding practices causing noise issues
bestPractices: Use star grounding to minimize ground loops
Implement input filters for conducted EMI
Provide adequate spacing for high-voltage isolation
Include protection diodes for reverse polarity
troubleshootingTips: If experiencing noise issues, check isolation and grounding
Overheating often caused by insufficient airflow in enclosures
Output ripple can be reduced with additional filtering

faqs.question: How do I handle ground loops in industrial systems?
faqs.answer: Ground loops can be minimized by: Using isolated DC-DC converters to break ground connections between subsystems; Implementing star grounding with a single ground point; Using differential signaling for long cable runs; Adding isolation barriers for sensitive analog circuits. Recom's isolated converters with 1600VDC+ isolation are ideal for breaking ground loops while maintaining signal integrity.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for grounding and isolation design recommendations.

faqs.question: What protection is needed for 24V industrial power?
faqs.answer: Industrial 24V systems need several protections: Reverse polarity protection - diode or MOSFET-based; Overvoltage protection - TVS diodes or crowbar circuits; Undervoltage lockout - prevents operation below minimum voltage; Short circuit protection - current limiting or foldback; Transient protection - TVS for surge suppression. Recom converters include built-in short circuit and overcurrent protection, but external protection may be needed for severe industrial transients.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for protection circuit design recommendations.

faqs.question: How do I select the right isolation voltage for my application?
faqs.answer: Isolation voltage selection depends on your application requirements: 1000VDC for basic industrial applications with low noise; 1600VDC for standard industrial grade with moderate noise immunity; 3000VDC for high isolation applications in harsh electrical environments; 6000VDC for maximum isolation in critical applications. Consider your system's working voltage, transient conditions, and safety requirements when selecting isolation voltage.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for isolation voltage selection guidance based on your specific application.

faqs.question: What is the MTBF of Recom industrial converters?
faqs.answer: Recom industrial converters feature excellent reliability with MTBF exceeding 1.5 million hours at 25°C ambient temperature. This translates to over 170 years of continuous operation. The high MTBF is achieved through conservative design, quality components, and rigorous manufacturing processes. Actual field reliability data shows failure rates well below 100 FIT (Failures In Time) under typical operating conditions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for detailed reliability data and MTBF calculations for your specific operating conditions.

faqs.question: Can Recom converters operate in high-vibration environments?
faqs.answer: Yes, Recom converters are designed to withstand industrial vibration and shock. Standard models meet IEC 60068-2-6 for vibration (5g, 10-500Hz) and IEC 60068-2-27 for shock (30g, 11ms). The SIP and DIP packages with through-hole mounting provide excellent mechanical stability. For extreme vibration environments, consider additional mechanical support or potting compounds.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for recommendations on vibration-resistant mounting and additional protection.

solutions.name: Medical Device Power Solution
solutions.title: Medical Device Power Solution
solutions.description: IEC 60601-1 compliant power system for patient-connected medical equipment
solutions.longDescription: The Medical Device Power Solution provides safe, reliable power for patient-connected medical equipment using Recom's medical-grade DC-DC converters and AC-DC power supplies. This solution meets IEC 60601-1 3rd edition safety standards with 2xMOPP isolation, low leakage current, and high isolation voltage. The modular design allows flexible configuration for various medical applications from diagnostic equipment to patient monitoring systems.
benefits: Low leakage current (<100μA) for CF-rated applications
Compact size enables portable medical device designs

coreAdvantages.title: Patient Safety
coreAdvantages.description: 2xMOPP (Means of Patient Protection) isolation provides redundant protection systems for patient-connected equipment.

coreAdvantages.title: Low Leakage
coreAdvantages.description: Leakage current below 100μA meets CF (cardiac floating) application requirements for direct patient contact.

coreAdvantages.title: High Isolation
coreAdvantages.description: 4000VAC or higher isolation voltage provides substantial safety margin for medical applications.

coreAdvantages.title: EMC Compliance
coreAdvantages.description: Meets EN 55011/CISPR 11 Class B for medical environments with minimal EMI emissions.

coreAdvantages.title: Compact Design
coreAdvantages.description: High power density enables portable and compact medical device designs without compromising safety.

components.description: 6W medical DC-DC converters for higher power

customerCases.challenge: Needed compact, battery-backed power for portable patient monitor with CF-rated patient connections and 8-hour battery life.
customerCases.solution: Implemented medical-grade AC-DC supply with 2xMOPP isolation and low leakage. Used high-efficiency DC-DC converters to maximize battery life.
customerCases.result: Passed IEC 60601-1 certification on first submission. Achieved 10-hour battery life, exceeding requirements. Device in production for 3 years with zero safety incidents.

customerCases.challenge: Required multiple isolated outputs for ultrasound transducers with strict leakage current limits and minimal EMI for image quality.
customerCases.solution: Designed distributed power architecture with medical-grade DC-DC converters providing isolated supplies for each transducer channel.
customerCases.result: Leakage current below 50μA, well under CF limits. EMI emissions 6dB below Class B limits. System deployed in 500+ hospitals worldwide.

faeInsights.insight: Medical power design requires meticulous attention to safety standards. The 2xMOPP requirement means you need two independent protection barriers between mains and patient. Recom's medical-grade converters are specifically designed with this in mind, with reinforced isolation and careful creepage/clearance design. Low leakage current is critical for CF-rated equipment - every component selection affects this.
faeInsights.logic: Medical power design follows strict safety hierarchy: First, AC-DC supply must provide 2xMOPP from mains to secondary. Second, DC-DC converters must maintain 2xMOPP for patient-connected circuits. Third, leakage current budget must be managed across all components. Fourth, EMC filtering must not compromise isolation. Fifth, all components must be medical-grade with appropriate certifications.
keyTakeaways: Leakage current must be budgeted across all components
Medical-grade components are essential for certification
EMC and safety requirements must be balanced
Documentation is critical for regulatory approval
commonPitfalls: Using non-medical grade components in patient circuits
Not accounting for all leakage current paths
Inadequate isolation between mains and patient
bestPractices: Use only IEC 60601-1 certified components for patient circuits
Design with 20% margin on leakage current limits
Document all isolation barriers for regulatory review
Test early and often with safety agency requirements
troubleshootingTips: High leakage current often caused by Y-capacitors - check values
Isolation failures usually due to creepage violations
EMI issues may require rebalancing filter components

faqs.question: What is 2xMOPP and why is it important?
faqs.answer: 2xMOPP (2 Means of Patient Protection) requires two independent protection systems between mains voltage and patient. This provides redundancy - if one protection fails, the second still protects the patient. For CF-rated equipment (direct cardiac connection), 2xMOPP is mandatory. Recom medical converters are designed with reinforced insulation providing 2xMOPP in a single component.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for medical safety standard guidance and 2xMOPP design requirements.

faqs.question: How do I calculate total leakage current?
faqs.answer: Total leakage current is the sum of all contributors: AC-DC supply leakage (typically 50-100μA); DC-DC converter leakage (10-50μA each); Filter capacitors (depends on value and voltage); Cable and PCB capacitance (typically small). For CF-rated equipment, total must be <100μA. Budget 80μA for components, leaving 20μA margin. Recom medical converters specify maximum leakage current to help with budgeting.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for leakage current calculation assistance and component selection.

faqs.question: What certifications do Recom medical products have?
faqs.answer: Recom medical products carry comprehensive certifications: IEC 60601-1 3rd Edition for medical electrical equipment; UL 60601-1 for North American markets; EN 60601-1 for European markets; CB scheme for international acceptance. All medical products are manufactured in ISO 13485 certified facilities and undergo 100% testing for isolation and leakage current.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact us for certification documentation and compliance support for your medical device.

faqs.question: How do I ensure EMC compliance in medical devices?
faqs.answer: Medical device EMC requires meeting both emissions and immunity standards: Emissions - EN 55011/CISPR 11 Class B for conducted and radiated emissions; Immunity - IEC 60601-1-2 for medical device immunity. Design practices include: Proper filtering on input and output; Shielded enclosures for sensitive circuits; Careful PCB layout with ground planes; Isolation barriers to prevent noise coupling. Recom medical converters are designed to meet Class B emissions with minimal external filtering.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for EMC design guidance and filtering recommendations.

faqs.question: What is the creepage and clearance requirement for medical devices?
faqs.answer: Creepage and clearance distances depend on working voltage and pollution degree: For 250V working voltage with 2xMOPP: Creepage minimum 8mm, Clearance minimum 5mm. These distances ensure no tracking or arcing occurs over the product lifetime. Recom medical converters are designed with enhanced creepage and clearance to meet these requirements. PCB layout must maintain these distances for all high-voltage paths.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for PCB layout guidance to meet creepage and clearance requirements.
